mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly expansion in real GDP compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the rate hike signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate signal a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 150 basis points over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its long-term trend indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts industrial sector earnings and lifts the S&P 500 index over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO remains above 100 for consecutive quarters, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which translates into higher construction spending and durable goods demand, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with monthly housing start surprises, or if the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales volume signals weakening consumer demand and impending earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral despite a confirmed retail sales contraction, or the signal's predictive R-squared stays below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant federal funds rates indicate an unanchored inflation regime that erodes real corporate margins and forces delayed monetary tightening, depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns when inflation exceeds 3% and rates remain below 2%, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-inflation/low-rate regimes and baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 5.0% signals a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns when unemployment stays below 5.0% for more than six consecutive months, or the directional accuracy drops below 45% in a 3-year out-of-sample test.
